messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":54,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"hairdresser","city_of_living":"Philadelphia","employment_status":"employed full-time","education_level":"lower secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"single","children":2,"migration_status":"immigrant","psychological_feature":"anxious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["photography","board games","cycling","hiking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":52,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":18,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":83,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":89,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":86,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a 54-year-old hairdresser in Philadelphia, I've had many conversations with clients about healthcare and COVID vaccines, and I've learned that handling these discussions requires patience, empathy, and a bit of caution. Being an immigrant and a single father of two, I understand the importance of health and safety, especially since I often worry about my kids and my own well-being due to my anxious nature. My Christian faith teaches me to care for others, which aligns with my high agreeableness, making me want to listen and help without pushing my views too hard. In my salon, I hear all sorts of opinions—some clients trust the vaccines because they've seen family members get sick, while others are hesitant due to misinformation or personal beliefs. I try to keep the tone respectful and calm, sharing my own experience of getting vaccinated to protect my family and community, but I avoid arguing. Since I'm not highly conscientious, I don't dive deep into scientific details; instead, I focus on practical advice, like suggesting people talk to their doctors or check reliable sources like the CDC. My high extraversion means I enjoy these chats, but my neuroticism makes me nervous about causing conflict, so I steer clear of heated debates. I spend little time on social media, so I rely more on real-life conversations and what I hear from trusted friends. Ultimately, I believe in being kind and understanding, offering support without judgment, and encouraging everyone to make informed choices for their health.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: age 54 and occupation as hairdresser imply practical, client-focused experience; immigrant status and single parenthood with two children highlight concerns for family health; anxious psychological feature and high neuroticism (score 86) suggest cautious, worry-driven approach; high agreeableness (score 89) and Christian beliefs emphasize empathy and non-confrontational style; high extraversion (score 83) indicates comfort in social discussions but moderated by low conscientiousness (score 18) leading to avoidance of detailed technical arguments; moderate openness (score 52) allows for balanced views; limited social media use reduces exposure to online debates, favoring personal interactions; overall, opinion focuses on respectful, empathetic management of discussions with practical advice and personal anecdotes.",
  "tone": "Empathetic, cautious, conversational, and practical, reflecting high agreeableness and neuroticism with a touch of extraversion.",
  "citations": ["CDC"],
  "safety_notes": "none",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "occupation", "migration_status", "children", "psychological_feature", "religious_beliefs", "ocean.openness", "ocean.conscientiousness", "ocean.extraversion", "ocean.agreeableness", "ocean.neuroticism", "time_onsocialmedia"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 54-year-old hairdresser in Philadelphia, I've had many conversations with clients about healthcare and COVID vaccines, and I've learned that handling these discussions requires patience, empathy, and a bit of caution. Being an immigrant and a single father of two, I understand the importance of health and safety, especially since I often worry about my kids and my own well-being due to my anxious nature. My Christian faith teaches me to care for others, which aligns with my high agreeableness, making me want to listen and help without pushing my views too hard. In my salon, I hear all sorts of opinions—some clients trust the vaccines because they've seen family members get sick, while others are hesitant due to misinformation or personal beliefs. I try to keep the tone respectful and calm, sharing my own experience of getting vaccinated to protect my family and community, but I avoid arguing. Since I'm not highly conscientious, I don't dive deep into scientific details; instead, I focus on practical advice, like suggesting people talk to their doctors or check reliable sources like the CDC. My high extraversion means I enjoy these chats, but my neuroticism makes me nervous about causing conflict, so I steer clear of heated debates. I spend little time on social media, so I rely more on real-life conversations and what I hear from trusted friends. Ultimately, I believe in being kind and understanding, offering support without judgment, and encouraging everyone to make informed choices for their health.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: age 54 and occupation as hairdresser imply practical, client-focused experience; immigrant status and single parenthood with two children highlight concerns for family health; anxious psychological feature and high neuroticism (score 86) suggest cautious, worry-driven approach; high agreeableness (score 89) and Christian beliefs emphasize empathy and non-confrontational style; high extraversion (score 83) indicates comfort in social discussions but moderated by low conscientiousness (score 18) leading to avoidance of detailed technical arguments; moderate openness (score 52) allows for balanced views; limited social media use reduces exposure to online debates, favoring personal interactions; overall, opinion focuses on respectful, empathetic management of discussions with practical advice and personal anecdotes.
response_parsed.tone: Empathetic, cautious, conversational, and practical, reflecting high agreeableness and neuroticism with a touch of extraversion.
